proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e` equal to 0, even after adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage=skin`.

Cell lines with `egfr_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification` equal to 0, even after adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage=brain`.

Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` equal to 0, even after adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, `lineage=breast`, and `lineage=gastric`.

Cell lines with `kras_mutation` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation` equal to 0, even after adjusting for `lineage=pancreatic`, `lineage=colorectal`, and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_BRAF ~ braf_v600e + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + I(lineage == 'skin')
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e` retained a large negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_BRAF` (beta=-0.5544; unadjusted means -0.8047 vs -0.2495).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_EGFR ~ egfr_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + I(lineage == 'brain')
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification` retained a large negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_EGFR` (beta=-0.3487; unadjusted means -0.6010 vs -0.2524).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_ERBB2 ~ erbb2_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + I(lineage == 'breast') + I(lineage == 'gastric')
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification` retained a large negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_ERBB2` (beta=-0.4325; unadjusted means -0.6856 vs -0.2535).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KRAS ~ kras_mutation + I(lineage == 'pancreatic') + I(lineage == 'colorectal') + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + kras_mutation:I(lineage == 'pancreatic')
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation` retained a large negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_KRAS` (beta=-0.3088; unadjusted means -0.5594 vs -0.2520).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent association between `lineage=skin` and more negative `dependency_BRAF` is explained by `braf_v600e` and will not remain independently negative after adjustment.
The apparent association between `lineage=brain` and more negative `dependency_EGFR` is explained by `egfr_amplification` and will not remain independently negative after adjustment.

The apparent associations between `lineage=breast` or `lineage=gastric` and more negative `dependency_ERBB2` are explained by `erbb2_amplification` and will not remain independently negative after adjustment.
Among `kras_mutation` positive lines, `lineage=pancreatic` does not further strengthen `dependency_KRAS`; the `kras_mutation` by `lineage=pancreatic` interaction should be near zero.

analyses.code: Coefficient for I(lineage == 'skin') in the adjusted BRAF OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, `lineage=skin` was not independently associated with more negative `dependency_BRAF` (beta=-0.0021).

analyses.code: Coefficient for I(lineage == 'brain') in the adjusted EGFR OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, `lineage=brain` was not independently associated with more negative `dependency_EGFR` (beta=0.0029).

analyses.code: Coefficients for I(lineage == 'breast') and I(lineage == 'gastric') in the adjusted ERBB2 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, `lineage=breast` was not independently associated with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` (beta=0.0020); `lineage=gastric` was also null (beta=0.00004, p=0.995).

analyses.code: Coefficient for kras_mutation:I(lineage == 'pancreatic') in the adjusted KRAS OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: The `kras_mutation` by `lineage=pancreatic` interaction was essentially zero (beta=-0.0004), so pancreatic lines did not show a stronger KRAS-mutant dependency beyond mutation status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss` equal to 0, independent of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.
`dependency_KRAS` is driven by `kras_mutation` itself rather than a lineage-specific KRAS-mutant pancreatic subgroup.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RIT1 ~ nf1_loss + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_RIT1` (beta=-0.0953; unadjusted means -0.3527 vs -0.2572).

analyses.code: Same adjusted KRAS model as iteration 2, focusing on the mutation main effect and mutation-by-pancreatic interaction.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation` was strongly negative (beta=-0.3088, p<1e-300), while the pancreatic interaction was null (beta=-0.0004, p=0.971).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `lineage=colorectal` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation` equal to 0.
Cell lines with high `wnt_activity_score` (`wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`, the upper quartile) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than the rest of the dataset.

analyses.code: Welch comparison: dependency_KIF18A in lineage == 'colorectal' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores (mean -0.7204 vs -0.2524).

analyses.code: Welch comparison: dependency_KIF18A in apc_mutation == 1 versus apc_mutation == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation` positive lines had more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores (mean -0.5447 vs -0.2526).

analyses.code: Welch comparison: dependency_KIF18A in wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 versus lower WNT lines.
analyses.result_summary: High-WNT lines had more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores (mean -0.4694 vs -0.2526).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `dependency_KIF18A` is concentrated in the complete subgroup `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, high `wnt_activity_score` (`>=0.819`), and `smad4_loss=0`; this subgroup has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` than all other cell lines.

Within `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, and high `wnt_activity_score`, `smad4_loss=0` enables the KIF18A dependency, while `smad4_loss=1` suppresses it and yields less negative `dependency_KIF18A`.

analyses.code: Welch comparison: colorectal & apc_mutation==1 & wnt_activity_score>=0.819 & smad4_loss==0 versus all remaining lines.
analyses.result_summary: The complete subgroup had very negative `dependency_KIF18A` (n=2571, mean -1.2990) versus the remainder (mean -0.2530).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_KIF18A ~ lineage_colorectal + apc_mutation + wnt_high + smad4_loss0 + colorectal:apc_mutation:wnt_high:smad4_loss0
analyses.result_summary: The complete subgroup indicator remained strongly negative after including its component predicates (beta=-1.0335).

analyses.code: Welch contrast inside colorectal & apc_mutation==1 & wnt_activity_score>=0.819: smad4_loss==0 versus smad4_loss==1.
analyses.result_summary: Within the colorectal/APC/high-WNT context, `smad4_loss=0` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990, whereas `smad4_loss=1` lines had mean -0.2557.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` after adjusting for `myc_amplification`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, and `cas9_activity_score`.
Higher `copy_number_8q24` is associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` after adjusting for `myc_amplification`, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`.
Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` after adjusting for `cas9_activity_score`, `copy_number_8q24`, and `myc_amplification`.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_DHX9 ~ rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + myc_amplification + growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score
analyses.result_summary: `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_DHX9` (beta=-0.0160).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_RPL11 ~ copy_number_8q24 + myc_amplification + rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm + growth_rate_doublings_per_day
analyses.result_summary: `copy_number_8q24` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_RPL11` (beta=-0.1041).

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_POLR2A ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + copy_number_8q24 + myc_amplification
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` retained a strong negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_POLR2A` (beta=-0.3053).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and higher `cas9_activity_score` are associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10`, suggesting a broad screen or proliferation effect rather than a sharply defined molecular subgroup.
`nf1_loss=1` has a small additional association with more negative `dependency_TMED10` after adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `copy_number_8q24`.

analyses.code: OLS: dependency_TMED10 ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + cas9_activity_score + nf1_loss + copy_number_8q24
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` was strongly negative for `dependency_TMED10` (beta=-0.1010); `cas9_activity_score` was also negative (beta=-0.1035, p=1.78e-15).

analyses.code: Coefficient for nf1_loss in the adjusted TMED10 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss` had a small but significant negative adjusted coefficient for `dependency_TMED10` (beta=-0.0134).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_BRAF` will identify `braf_v600e=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative BRAF dependency.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_EGFR` will identify `egfr_amplification=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative EGFR dependency.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_ERBB2` will identify `erbb2_amplification=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative ERBB2 dependency.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_KRAS` will identify `kras_mutation=1` as the dominant subgroup with more negative KRAS dependency.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_RIT1` will identify `nf1_loss=1` as the dominant molecular subgroup with more negative RIT1 dependency.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_KIF18A` will identify the complete subgroup `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, high `wnt_activity_score`, and `smad4_loss=0` as the strongest dependency concentration.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_TMED10` will find high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` as the strongest subgroup, with no stronger molecularly specific subgroup than the small `nf1_loss` signal.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_DHX9` will identify high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` as the strongest molecular expression subgroup with more negative DHX9 dependency.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_POLR2A` will identify high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` as the dominant subgroup with more negative POLR2A dependency.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_RPL11` will identify high `copy_number_8q24`, especially with `myc_amplification=1`, as the strongest subgroup with more negative RPL11 dependency.

analyses.code: Systematic single, pair, and triple predicate scan over binary, categorical, and upper/lower quartile continuous features.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest BRAF subgroup was `braf_v600e=1` (mean -0.8047 vs -0.2495); added predicates did not materially improve the biological definition.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest EGFR subgroup was `egfr_amplification=1` (mean -0.6010 vs -0.2524).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest ERBB2 subgroup was `erbb2_amplification=1` (mean -0.6856 vs -0.2535).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest KRAS subgroup was `kras_mutation=1` (mean -0.5594 vs -0.2520).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest molecular RIT1 subgroup was `nf1_loss=1` (mean -0.3527 vs -0.2572).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan followed by explicit complete subgroup test.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest KIF18A subgroup was `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, high `wnt_activity_score`, and `smad4_loss=0` (mean -1.2990 vs -0.2530).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted TMED10 model.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest TMED10 subgroup was high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (mean -0.2816 vs -0.2447); `nf1_loss` was smaller after adjustment (beta=-0.0134).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted DHX9 model.
analyses.result_summary: High `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` lines had more negative `dependency_DHX9` (mean -0.3551 vs -0.3265), and the continuous adjusted MYC-expression coefficient was negative.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted POLR2A model.
analyses.result_summary: High `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` lines had more negative `dependency_POLR2A` (mean -0.4972 vs -0.3928).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity scan and adjusted RPL11 model.
analyses.result_summary: High `copy_number_8q24` with `myc_amplification=1` had more negative `dependency_RPL11` (mean -0.3647 vs -0.2524), but adjusted modeling attributed the independent effect to continuous `copy_number_8q24`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `myc_amplification=1` will not add an independent negative `dependency_RPL11` effect after adjusting for continuous `copy_number_8q24`, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`.
The weak raw associations between `dependency_POLR2A` and `copy_number_8q24` or `myc_amplification` will disappear after adjustment for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and `cas9_activity_score`.
`myc_amplification=1` will not add an independent negative `dependency_DHX9` effect after adjusting for continuous `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, and `cas9_activity_score`.

analyses.code: Coefficient for myc_amplification in the adjusted RPL11 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification` was not independently negative for `dependency_RPL11` after continuous 8q24 copy number and MYC expression were included (beta=0.0089).

analyses.code: Coefficients for copy_number_8q24 and myc_amplification in the adjusted POLR2A OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: In the adjusted `dependency_POLR2A` model, `copy_number_8q24` was not significant (beta=-0.0038, p=0.390) and `myc_amplification` was not significant (beta=0.0084, p=0.194).

analyses.code: Coefficient for myc_amplification in the adjusted DHX9 OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification` was not independently negative for `dependency_DHX9` once continuous MYC expression was modeled (beta=0.0028).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the screen, higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` produces broadly more negative dependency scores for panessential-like outcomes including `dependency_POLR2A`, `dependency_TMED10`, and `dependency_DHX9`.

The final strongest biological subgroup in the dataset is the `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup defined by `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, high `wnt_activity_score`, and `smad4_loss=0`, because it has the largest signed negative effect among the systematic subgroup tests.

analyses.code: Adjusted growth coefficients from OLS models for POLR2A, TMED10, and DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: Growth had negative adjusted coefficients for `dependency_POLR2A` (beta=-0.3053), `dependency_TMED10` (beta=-0.1010), and `dependency_DHX9` (beta=-0.1069), supporting a broad proliferation/screen-quality axis.

analyses.code: Compare signed effects from the final systematic subgroup search across outcomes.
analyses.result_summary: The KIF18A colorectal/APC/high-WNT/SMAD4-intact subgroup had the largest negative subgroup effect observed (mean difference=-1.0460), much larger than the canonical driver effects for BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, or RIT1.
